reviews.review_msg: I strongly advise against taking his class. The lectures are just pointless student discussions with zero real teaching. The material is hard, and he does nothing to help. It's like he doesn't care if we succeed. If I'd known how pointless this class would be, I'd have dropped it immediately
Doesn't know how to teach. I had to rely on YouTube videos to get me through this class
Rude and dismissive of students. Lectures were pretty boring

Ive had him for a couple classes and I was kinda happy I had him for this one. He is very understanding and actually wants success for his students. Go to class because phil papers are difficult to understand properly. Marking is reasonable. Great with articulating ideas and delivery. Likes to talk and likes people in general.

Truly the best professor I've ever had. Prof Begby is a fantastic lecturer, very understanding, and accessible outside of class. He gives great advice during office hours and is always willing to explain things in greater detail when you don't understand! Could not recommend enough.

Endre is one of the best profs I've had. While lectures can be bland sometimes, he gives great feedback and is very open during office hours to provide any help for papers. A reasonable marker too. Definitely recommend taking a class with Endre no matter what the subject is.
Endre is a good prof and connects a lot of the material regarding other courses that he teaches. But his teaching is so dry. Easy course though if you get what he is saying!

slides were too long and repetitive but his teaching style is nice. Tries to engage with students. Assignments are easy as long as you have an idea of what he's talking about, use examples from his slides. 5 quizzes with unlimited retakes, but only for once a week. Writing assignments are simple and straightforward and can be done maybe 30 minutes.

This class was nice and chill, very easy to follow along. I would recommend keeping up with the readings as they help cement the concepts in your mind, but everything you are tested on is in the lectures. 50% of your grade is based on 5 in class quizzes that have retake opportunities, and half of the 20% final was made of recycled quiz questions.
Oh this class was so boring, i had to grab a coffee every time I went to his class.

I re-took this course a year later with Meg Grant. So many things Endre taught were incorrect and the course was completely different. Endre teaches hardly anything compared to other professors and is so boring. Wait for anybody else to take this course with. Endre seems alright until you take the course with someone who actually knows the content.

Came into this course not expecting much but it actually turned out pretty interesting! Professor Begby is nice and he posts lecture notes for each lecture too, which helped a lot. Course consisted of two papers, participation, and a take-home final, which was alright and mainly got you to more thoroughly analyze some of the readings.
Very good lecturer, and good supplementary materials/notes available if you aren't able to make class. Very light work load.

Very nice prof, I like him as a person. Slides are disorganized and unnecessarily long. Kind of boring, I find it hard to pay attention to him. Quizzes are unlimited repeats and barely change each time so it's nice. Easy to do good on assignments as long as you know what you need to do.

genuinely one of the best professors i've ever had. dr. begby is a super engaging lecturer and ensures that everyone is clear on what he is explaining. really understanding and always willing to help in office hours. likes to really get into discussions, especially if you show an interest! encourages participation and is just an overall great guy.

The man!!!! coolest professor, absolute genius. encourages student participation and treats you as an intellectual equal while still making sure you leave knowing more. Super super nice, really funny, and very helpful during office hours, especially when developing your arguments for your paper. overall best prof, and pretty cool course. take him!

This rating is not so much about the professor as it is about epistemology. It is a very weird phenomenon that never once mentions 'wisdom' as a concept, hence, the subject material consists of trite machinations of what to believe, how to arrive there around some tacit assumption of the existence of morality. Follow the rules is how to pass

Cannot say enough good things about this guy! Literally the best professor I have ever had. Cares about all of his students and makes sure everyone is following and feeling good about the content. Interesting lectures and encourages participation, plus super helpful guiding paper writing. Funny too! Overall, fantastic prof!!
His classes are fun and he's very charming! Everything you need is in the slides (and there aren't even that many of them)! Take his class.

Endre's class is pretty easy because 50% of your grade is made up of quizzes that you get multiple retakes on, it's also worth noting that most of the times the quizzes are barely even changed. I also noticed he recycled some quiz questions in the final so if you are able know all the right answers for the quiz you should have an easy enough time.

The best prof for COGS 100, his lectures are very important and always engaged the entire class. He knows his stuff and poses very important topics that require a lot of thought. The final was very doable and his assignments were also very helpful in understanding important topics needed for the weekly quizzes and final. Take him if you can!!!

He's such a chill prof. Lectures are pretty interesting and easy to follow along. It has everything you need. The topics can be very abstract at times but the class gets better as you progress. Grades were very fair with final, quizzes (retries allowed) and writing assignments that aren't too bad if you go to lecture. Readings are very long.

AMAZING prof! He cares a lot about the class, knows the material very well, and has formatted this course in a great manner. In-class quizzes make up 50 percent of the course but he lets you re-do them, assignments are very straightforward and he helps explain concepts very well! He may very well be the best prof I have had in a really long time!

One of the best classes I've taken at SFU and all credit goes to Dr. Begby for that. Engaging lectures and really interesting content. Super accommodating prof who genuinely cares about his students. He also has a great sense of humor. Definitely recommend taking this class with Begby!

If this wasn't my last semester I definitely would've dropped the class the first week of lectures because the content just seemed SO confusing and I had no idea what was going on, but I swear it starts making sense and is so interesting too! Great prof, very organized & loved the structure of the class

Endre is funny, kind and approachable. Everything you need to know is on his slides and his presentations are concise.

There are 5 quizzes worth 10% each that you can retake multiple times, there are 5 writings worth 6% and there's a final worth 20%. Super easy to do well and enjoy this course with this grading scheme and his awesome teaching!

Dr. Begby is awesome. Cognitive Science can be overwhelming when you're first introduced to it but he explains concepts so clearly that you end up learning it way better than you thought you did! He's also hilarious, super helpful in office hours, and overall a great prof!! This course was super interesting too, cannot recommend more!
Weekly quizzes, assignments and 3 papers with no final exam. Lectures can be a bit boring, but the content is interesting.

TAKE A CLASS WITH HIM IF YOU CAN! Dr. Begby is a great prof. He really wants you to know the course info and if you listen to him, you will learn. Aside from being a cool dude, he is also super knowledgeable in philosophy and cognition. Although because he's super knowledgable sometimes you feel stupid when you ask him a question. He's cool though.

Easily my favourite prof at SFU. He's super kind, approachable and willing to accommodate to students' needs. Puts a lot of effort into making lectures interesting and gives you lots of chances to gain points for participation (e.g. discussion board and live lectures). All the marking is done by the TA, but Endre still helps if you need it. 10/10.

Great prof! He cares about his students and is very accommodating during the time of online learning. The course isn't tough, a lot of material to learn but if you listen in the lectures you should be fine. There are quizzes every other week or so that are worth 10% each, but he allows you to have retake opportunities which is nice.

What a nice prof! He really cares about the class and the learning experience. He encourages questions and discussions during class which is really cool. I didn't really ask questions myself or comment but it was fun to see what my classmates had to say. My only complaint is that he was a bit monotonous sometimes so it was hard to keep focused.

COGS 100 Professor Begby is an overall great guy. He values true learning. He is definitely one of the best professors at SFU. He genuinely cares about students and is always accessible. He also gives good feedback so that you can improve. He is very friendly, helpful, and approachable, and seems to genuinely enjoy teaching. 10/10 recommend.
Great prof! Despite lectures being all on zoom, Dr. Begby did a great job of conveying course material. 10/10 would recommend

Endre is an overall really solid prof! Generally really encouraging, and he really enjoys hearing students' perspective on philosophy. He's not an easy grader by any means, but he gives good feedback so that you can improve. He doesn't have slides for online classes, so be prepared to talk a lot in class discussions

He's genuinely the most caring professor out there, and always down to converse and answer any questions asked. Participation matters in tutorials and there's quite a lot of mini in-class assignments and a few papers but they are very straightforward. Be sure to read the material he provides for better understanding of the content.

Begby is a nice guy overall but his lectures were excruciatingly dry and boring, I took this class as a GPA booster because I thought the material looked interesting but boy do I regret it; his marking is fair but his only TA marks way too hard for a first year class and often made condescending remarks about how she was grumpy while marking

Endre's class was one of my favourites this semester! He was very good at making everything clear, even things that I had previously been unfamiliar with. He welcomes class participation and really cares about his students, even making the effort to get to know them on a more personal level. Would highly recommend taking 121 with him!

Knows his material. He is super knowledgeable about his field. There is no textbook, just free, accessible online readings which is extremely respectable. There are 3 papers and a final exam, a fair bit of writing but not super difficult to achieve a good grade. Overall really interesting course, would definitely recommend it.

The class did have a lot of writing, - multiple assignments and 3 papers ranging from 1 page to 6 pages. However, Endre made the class very interesting . Attending lecture made the content a lot easier to understand, and he provided very in depth and eye opening insight on a variety of social issues.
good prof., encourages students to ask questions

Favourite professor + a really interesting class! He loves when people ask questions, and encourages class discussions. If you are looking for a GPA booster, don't take this class. At the beginning of the semester, my TA said they don't really give A's.

I have taken 2 courses with Endre. He's great at presenting a topic from various angles and making everyone understand all the relevant issues. I was very satisfied with the topics of discussion in this course.

Amazing class! Endre is definitely one of the best professors at SFU. Have also taken 467 and 344 with him and he has been fantastic in all 3. Great at getting students interested and engaged, great lecturer, and gives helpful feedback. Genuinely cares about his students and very willing to meet outside of class.
Super nice guy. Great speaker. Lots of reading

Such an intellectual man. His lectures make it really easy to understand the material. Attend all lectures!! They will really help you succeed. TA was a bit of a tough marker though. Course material is very valuable and there isn't too much work. Weekly readings

One of the best professors I have had. The course content was already very interesting, but Endre Begby has a way of making you question it all without being confused. He's also very friendly, helpful, and approachable, and seems to genuinely enjoy teaching. I would definitely recommend the course with him to everyone!

Endre explains the material very well. Personally, I found philosophy of language a bit harder than other courses but I still got a good general overview of the relevant issues from going to lectures. He is also surprising chill and fun to talk to.

Best Prof!!! He is also very approachable. I enjoyed his Epistemology course and loved Ethics of War! He does a GREAT job simplifying complex ideas and also really engages the class to understand the material. None of the material is easy but at the end of the course you'll be amazed how well you understand it!  BONUS: no textbook, just handouts

Has the unique ability to let a class struggle with a difficult issue while keeping it firmly on track. Always on-topic and insightful discussions. Readings are challenging, but hey, it's philosophy. He does a great job of highlighting what he thinks is important without going over every detail. Creates a safe environment to try out ideas.
I  can only repeat it "get ready to read".  Was a fantastic survey of epistemology.

The irritating know-it-all of the class ruined what could have been a lot more group discussion. When someone cannot fathom that sharing their thoughts and opinions multiple times per lecture is superfluous, it hinders fellow classmates. Overall Begby is a great professor, however you may experience an unfortunate group due to the small class size.

Truly believes that what he says is useful and worth thinking about. The course covers material from historical nonsense to his own nonsense. Assigns papers that refuse to acknowledge that science has already proven their own claims wrong. Ends the course with social epistemology; You know, social psychology without the science or experiments.

Good reading selection, and you actually learn a ton about moral philosophy in lecture. The papers are difficult to write if you aren't the best writer or aren't acquainted with philosophy papers. Tough marker, actually makes you think and argue concisely. I'd recommend taking this course to anyone, valuable information imo. Not really a GPA boost.

One of the best profs ever! Even though phil 120 is a writing course, his papers are short, and actually interesting to write about. the final was great, as long as you attended lectures and had the general idea of what each philosopher had to say, you're golden. Possible GPA booster, and I'm not even good at writing.
